160	\begin{definition}
161	
162	\leanok
163	[Structure morphism of the relative spectrum]
164	  \label{def:relspec_structure_morphism}
165	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.structureMorphism}
166	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_exists, def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
167	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\mathcal{A}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra.
168	  The \emph{structure morphism}
169	  \(\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X\) is the canonical projection of the
170	  relative spectrum to its base, obtained as the colimit descent of the affine-local
171	  structure morphisms \(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \to U\) over the directed affine open
172	  cover of \(X\). It is the morphism whose affineness and base-change behaviour are the
173	  subject of the universal-property and base-change results below.
174	\end{definition}
175	
176	\begin{proof}
177	
178	\leanok
179	  The structure morphism is the base morphism of the relative gluing data of
180	  \(\mathcal{A}\); it is the projection underlying \cref{thm:relative_spec_exists}.
181	\end{proof}

378	  \(\mathcal{A}\) gives \(\mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A}\) as \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
379	  (with the \(R\)-algebra structure on \(A\) inducing the algebra structure on
380	  \(\widetilde A\)). The ring map \(R \to A\) gives a morphism
381	  \(f_{\mathrm{univ}} : \Spec A \to \Spec R = X\) and a canonical
382	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A}\)-algebra map

384	  \to \mathcal{O}_{\Spec A} = \widetilde A$ given by multiplication
385	  \(a \otimes a' \mapsto aa'\). We verify \((\Spec A, f_{\mathrm{univ}}, \varphi_{\mathrm{univ}})\)

387	  \((f, \varphi)\), the composite

398	\section{Lean encoding}
399	\label{sec:relspec_lean_encoding}
400	
401	The Lean target file is the new \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean}.
402	The construction proceeds by gluing absolute affine pieces: for each affine open
403	\(U \subseteq X\) with \(U = \Spec R\) and \(A := \mathcal{A}(U)\) regarded as an
404	\(R\)-algebra, the local piece is \(\Spec A\) with structure morphism \(\Spec A \to \Spec R = U\)
405	induced by the algebra unit \(R \to A\). Mathlib provides \texttt{IsAffineOpen.toScheme} for
406	the affine-open inclusion and the absolute \texttt{Scheme.Spec} functor for the local
407	pieces; the transition isomorphisms come from quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{A}\) via
408	\texttt{QuasiCoherent.tensorIso} (or the explicit
409	\(\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)\) for \(V = \Spec S \subseteq U\)).
410	The gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData} (general construction) or
411	the convenience wrapper \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme.glueOpens}; the cocycle
412	condition reduces to Stacks \texttt{lemma-transitive-spec}. The universal property
413	(\cref{thm:relative_spec_univ}) is expressed as a
414	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} witness, providing the natural bijection
415	$\Hom_X(T, \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})) \cong
416	\Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X\text{-alg}}(\mathcal{A}, g_*\mathcal{O}_T)$ as a
417	\texttt{Yoneda}-style structure. No new core axiom is required; the entire chapter is a
418	definitional/structural construction on top of existing Mathlib affine-scheme
419	infrastructure.
420	
421	\section{Out of scope}
422	\label{sec:relspec_out_of_scope}
423	
424	This chapter packages \emph{only} the relative-spectrum construction and its core
425	properties. The following downstream constructions live in separate sibling chapters and
426	are explicitly out of scope here:
427	\begin{itemize}
428	  \item \textbf{Relative Picard functor \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\)} (A.1.c) --- consumed-by, not
429	    defined-here; its blueprint home is the planned chapter
430	    \texttt{Picard\_RelPicFunctor.tex}.
431	  \item \textbf{Line-bundle pullback on a product \(C \times_k T\)} (A.1.b) ---
432	    \texttt{Picard\_LineBundlePullback.tex} (planned).
433	  \item \textbf{Connectedness, dimensionality, smoothness of \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\)}
434	    --- downstream properties that depend on assumptions on \(\mathcal{A}\)
435	    (locally finite type, flat, etc.), not unconditional consequences of the construction.
436	  \item \textbf{Identity-component \(\Pic^0\) and abelian-variety structure} (A.3) --- a
437	    separate Route~A sub-row, fed by but not constructed in this chapter.
438	\end{itemize}

5	% STRATEGY NOTE. This chapter packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
6	% Grothendieck--Altman--Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
7	% \(\mathrm{Quot}^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\): the per-fiber Hilbert polynomial, the
8	% Quot functor of \(T\)-flat coherent quotients of \(E_T\) on
9	% \(X_T = X \times_S T\), and the Grassmannian \emph{scheme} together with
10	% its representability. The Quot functor underlies the relative Picard
11	% functor via the Hilbert scheme \(\Hilb_{C/k} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_C/C/k}\)
12	% -- the case \(E = \mathcal{O}_X\) of the Quot functor.
13	%
14	% LOAD-BEARING SUB-PREREQUISITE. The Quot construction embeds Quot as a
15	% locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian
16	% \(\mathrm{Grass}(W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))\).
17	% Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} -- only
18	% a linear-algebra Grassmannian as a finite-rank-quotient variety. The
19	% scheme-level Grassmannian over an arbitrary noetherian base is therefore an
20	% in-project sub-build. This chapter introduces it as
21	% \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme} and states its representability
22	% (\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).

24	\section{Setup and motivation}
25	\label{sec:quot_setup}
26	
27	Throughout this chapter, let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a
28	projective morphism, \(L\) a relatively very ample line bundle on \(X\) over \(S\),
29	and \(E\) a coherent sheaf on \(X\). The Quot construction packages the
30	\(T\)-flat coherent quotients \(E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) of the
31	pull-back \(E_T\) to \(X_T = X \times_S T\), modulo the equivalence
32	\(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle\) defined by
33	\(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\). Fixing the Hilbert polynomial
34	\(\Phi(\lambda) = \chi(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}(\lambda)) \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\)
35	(constant on connected \(T\) by flatness)
36	stratifies the Quot functor into pieces \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\), each of
37	which Grothendieck proved is representable by a projective \(S\)-scheme.
38	This chapter develops the foundations of that picture: the Hilbert
39	polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}), the Quot functor
40	(\cref{def:quot_functor}), and the Grassmannian scheme
41	(\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme}) with its representability
42	(\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).
43	
44	% SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1;
45	% FGA Explained Ch.~5, AMS MSM 123 (read from
46	% references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L287-L500).
47	% The Hartshorne reference for the polynomial-eventually
48	% property of Euler characteristics is [Hartshorne], III.5.2 (the scanned
49	% PDF at references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf has no text layer, so
50	% the verbatim source-quote is taken from
51	% Nitsure \S 1; Hartshorne III.5.2 is cited in prose as the textbook
52	% reference).
53	
54	\section{Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf}
55	\label{sec:hilbert_polynomial}
56	
57	\begin{definition}\leanok
58	[Hilbert polynomial]
59	  \label{def:hilbert_polynomial}
60	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial}
61	  \uses{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}
62	  % ENCODING. `def:hilbert_polynomial` is formalized via the GRADED HILBERT
63	  % FUNCTION of the section module ⊕_{m≥0} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})
64	  % (def:sectionGradedModule), whose polynomiality is extracted in
65	  % thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule from the graded Hilbert–Serre rationality
66	  % lemma (lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational) together with the Mathlib lemma
67	  % Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly (lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib). The
68	  % construction uses only H^0; for m≫0 it agrees with the Euler characteristic
69	  % χ by Serre vanishing, so it is the classical Hilbert polynomial and no
70	  % numerical invariant is lost.
71	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
72	  % Polynomials" (read from
73	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L478).
74	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
75	  % "Let \(X\) be a finite type scheme over
76	  %  a field \(k\), together with a line bundle \(L\).
77	  %  Recall that if \(F\) is a coherent sheaf on
78	  %  \(X\) whose support is proper over \(k\), then
79	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} \({\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]\) of \(F\) is defined by
80	  %  the function
81	  %  \(\){\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
82	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})\(\)
83	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
84	  %  coherence and properness conditions. The fact that \(\chi(F(m))\)
85	  %  is indeed a polynomial in \(m\) under the above assumption is a special
86	  %  case of what is known as Snapper's Lemma (see Kleiman [K] for a proof).
87	  %
88	  %  Let \(X\to S\) be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
89	  %  and let \(L\) be a line bundle on \(X\). Let \(\F\)
90	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
91	  %  \(X\) whose schematic support is proper over \(S\). Then for each \(s\in S\),
92	  %  we get a polynomial \({\Phi}_s \in  \Q[\lambda]\)
93	  %  which is the Hilbert polynomial of
94	  %  the restriction \(\F_s = F|_{X_s}\) of \(\F\) to the fiber \(X_s\) over \(s\),
95	  %  calculated with respect to the line bundle \(L_s = L|_{X_s}\).
96	  %  If \(\F\) is flat over \(S\) then the function \(s\mapsto {\Phi}_s\)
97	  %  from the set of points of \(S\) to the polynomial ring \(\Q[\lambda]\) is
98	  %  known to be locally constant on \(S\)."
99	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ [Hartshorne],
100	  III.5.2.}
101	  Let \(X \to S\) be a finite-type morphism of noetherian schemes and let \(L\)
102	  be a line bundle on \(X\). For a coherent sheaf \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) whose
103	  schematic support is proper over \(S\) and a point \(s \in S\), write \(X_s\)
104	  for the fibre over \(s\), \(\mathcal{F}_s = \mathcal{F}|_{X_s}\) and
105	  \(L_s = L|_{X_s}\). The \emph{Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}\) at \(s\)
106	  relative to \(L\)} is the unique polynomial
107	  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\) that agrees, for all
108	  \(m \gg 0\), with the graded Hilbert function
109	  \[
110	    m \;\longmapsto\; \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr)
111	  \]
112	  of the section module
113	  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\)
114	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}), viewed as a finitely generated graded module
115	  over the homogeneous section ring
116	  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\)
117	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}). Existence and uniqueness of
118	  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) are the content of
119	  \cref{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}.
120	
121	  This is the classical Hilbert polynomial. When \(\mathcal{F}_s\) is coherent
122	  and \(L_s\) is ample on the proper \(\kappa(s)\)-scheme \(X_s\), Serre vanishing
123	  gives \(H^i(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}) = 0\) for all \(i > 0\)
124	  and \(m \gg 0\), so for \(m \gg 0\)
125	  \[
126	    \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
127	    \;=\; \chi(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
128	    \;=\; \sum_{i \ge 0} (-1)^i \dim_{\kappa(s)} H^i(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}),
129	  \]
130	  and hence \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) coincides with the Euler-characteristic
131	  Hilbert polynomial of Snapper's Lemma -- no numerical invariant is lost. The
132	  construction above, however, uses only the global sections \(H^0\): the higher
133	  cohomology enters solely through the classical statement that the two functions
134	  agree for \(m \gg 0\), never through the definition of \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\)
135	  itself. When \(\mathcal{F}\) is \(S\)-flat the function
136	  \(s \mapsto \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is locally constant on \(S\), so \(\Phi\) is
137	  well-defined on connected components.
138	\end{definition}
139	
140	\section{Graded Hilbert polynomial}
141	\label{sec:graded_hilbert_polynomial}
142	
143	This section gives the graded-Hilbert-function construction that underlies
144	\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Working over a single fibre \(X_s\) with its
145	ample line bundle \(L_s\) and coherent sheaf \(\mathcal{F}_s\), it builds the
146	section graded ring (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) and module
147	(\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}), records their finite generation
148	(\cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg}), and extracts the Hilbert polynomial
149	(\cref{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}) by combining the graded
150	Hilbert--Serre rationality of the Hilbert series
151	(\cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}) with Mathlib's polynomial-extraction
152	lemma (\cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}). The route uses only \(H^0\)
153	(global sections), so it is independent of higher coherent cohomology.
154	
155	\begin{definition}
156	[Section graded ring]
157	  \label{def:sectionGradedRing}
158	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedRing}
159	  % NOTE: Formalization is blocked on the absence of tensor products for sheaves of modules in
160	  % Mathlib at the pinned commit. The degree-\(m\) component \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\)
161	  % requires a tensor power \(L_s^{\otimes m}\) of a line bundle; this infrastructure (tensor
162	  % product of sheaves of modules with lax-monoidal global sections) is not yet available, making
163	  % this definition a prerequisite sub-build.
164	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
165	  % sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert Polynomials" (read from
166	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
167	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
168	  % "Let $X$ be a finite type scheme over
169	  %  a field $k$, together with a line bundle $L$.
170	  %  Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
171	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
172	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
173	  %  the function
174	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
175	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
176	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
177	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
178	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); the section graded
179	  ring is the homogeneous coordinate ring of [Hartshorne], I.7.}
180	  Let \(s \in S\) and let \(L_s\) be a line bundle on the fibre \(X_s\), a scheme
181	  of finite type over the residue field \(\kappa(s)\). The \emph{section graded
182	  ring} of \(L_s\) is the graded commutative ring
183	  \[
184	    R(X_s, L_s) \;=\; \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr),
185	  \]
186	  with degree-\(m\) component \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\) and multiplication
187	  the tensor product of sections
188	  \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m}) \otimes_{\kappa(s)}
189	  \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m'}) \to \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes (m+m')})\),
190	  \(\sigma \otimes \tau \mapsto \sigma \cdot \tau\). Its degree-zero component is
191	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{O}_{X_s})\), which contains \(\kappa(s)\). When \(X_s\) is
192	  proper over \(\kappa(s)\) and \(L_s\) is ample, \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely
193	  generated -- hence Noetherian -- graded \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra (a suitable
194	  Veronese power of \(L_s\) is very ample and embeds \(X_s\) into a projective
195	  space, exhibiting \(R(X_s, L_s)\) up to a finite extension as a quotient of a
196	  polynomial ring).
197	\end{definition}

199	\begin{definition}
200	[Section graded module]

949	  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\). Applying the locality criterion
950	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} to this family of per-open
951	  isomorphisms shows that the base-change map is an isomorphism, as asserted.
952	\end{proof}

954	\subsection{The section-level base-change identity}
955	
956	The section-level identification
957	Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange} below is the single
958	highest-leverage obligation of the affine close. In the affine--affine model the
959	base-change map \(\theta\) is, after its domain and codomain are read through the two
960	proved tilde dictionaries, a concrete \(R'\)-linear map
961	\(R' \otimes_R M \to (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\), and the content of the lemma is
962	that this map is the inverse of the cancellation isomorphism
963	\(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\). Because the base-change map is \emph{defined} as the
964	transpose of the inner pushforward comparison \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) under the
965	\((g^* \dashv g_*)\)-adjunction (\(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\)), its section-level value
966	is read off by the \emph{conjugate-counit calculus}: unfolding the transpose through the
967	counit form of the hom-set adjunction
968	(\(\operatorname{Adjunction.homEquiv\_counit}\)) factors \(\theta\) as
969	\(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\) followed by the geometric counit \(\varepsilon_g\), and
970	conjugating this composite by the two tilde dictionaries
971	\(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}) and
972	\(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) replaces the
973	geometric counit by the \emph{algebraic} extension-of-scalars counit along \(\psi\). This
974	is the route the live formalization takes: the cancellation identity
975	Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange} routes through the generator
976	trace Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace}, which is a one-line corollary of the
977	section identity Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_section_identity}, whose single residual
978	obligation is the counit-conjugate coherence Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}.
979	The per-generator/element evaluation is \emph{not} available as a shortcut: evaluating the
980	opaque geometric composite on \(1 \otimes m\) produces no element-level normal form that
981	\(\operatorname{rfl}\) or \(\operatorname{simp}\) can reduce, so the abstract conjugate
982	calculus is the only tractable discharge. Two Mathlib results are recorded
983	first as dependency anchors.
984	
985	\begin{lemma}[Pullback of a pair of affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps]
986	  \label{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}
987	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackSpecIso}
988	  \mathlibok
989	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
990	  Let \(R \to S\) and \(R \to T\) be \(R\)-algebras. Then the fibre product of the
991	  induced morphisms \(\operatorname{Spec}(S) \to \operatorname{Spec}(R)\) and
992	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(T) \to \operatorname{Spec}(R)\) is canonically the spectrum of
993	  the tensor product,
994	  \[
995	    \operatorname{Limits.pullback}\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}(R\to S),\,
996	      \operatorname{Spec}(R\to T)\bigr)
997	    \;\cong\; \operatorname{Spec}\bigl(S \otimes_R T\bigr)
998	    \qquad (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackSpecIso}),
999	  \]
1000	  and under this isomorphism the two cone legs are the \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps of
1001	  the canonical tensor inclusions: \texttt{pullbackSpecIso\_hom\_fst} identifies the
1002	  first projection with \(\operatorname{Spec}\) of
1003	  \(\texttt{Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom} : S \to S \otimes_R T\), and
1004	  \texttt{pullbackSpecIso\_hom\_snd} identifies the second projection with
1005	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\) of
1006	  \(\texttt{Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight} : T \to S \otimes_R T\) (with companion
1007	  forms \texttt{pullbackSpecIso\_inv\_fst}, \texttt{\_inv\_snd}).
1008	\end{lemma}

1249	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1250	[Pullback along an isomorphism is an equivalence of module categories]
1251	  \label{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1252	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1253	  Let \(f : X \to Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes. Then the pullback functor on
1254	  \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is
1255	  one half of an equivalence of categories
1256	  \(Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \simeq X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\), with quasi-inverse
1257	  \((f^{-1})^*\).
1258	\end{lemma}
1259	\begin{proof}
1260	
1261	  Assemble the equivalence from the functor \(f^*\) and the candidate inverse
1262	  \((f^{-1})^*\). The unit and counit isomorphisms are built from the pseudofunctor
1263	  coherences of pullback: \((f^{-1})^* \circ f^* \cong (f \circ f^{-1})^* = \mathrm{id}^*
1264	  \cong \mathrm{id}\) and symmetrically, using
1265	  \(f \circ f^{-1} = \mathrm{id}_Y\), \(f^{-1} \circ f = \mathrm{id}_X\)
1266	  (composition-of-pullbacks, congruence under equal maps, and pullback-of-identity).
1267	  The triangle identities are forced by these coherences.
1268	\end{proof}
1269	
1270	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1271	[The pullback functor along an isomorphism is an equivalence]
1272	  \label{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
1273	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
1274	  \uses{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1275	  Consequently, for an isomorphism \(f : X \to Y\) the functor
1276	  \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is an equivalence of
1277	  categories. In particular its adjunction unit is a natural isomorphism, so
1278	  \((\eta_{f})_{W}\) is invertible on every object \(W\).
1279	\end{lemma}
1280	\begin{proof}
1281	
1282	  The functor underlying the equivalence of
1283	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso} is exactly \(f^*\), so \(f^*\) is an
1284	  equivalence; an equivalence's unit is a natural isomorphism.
1285	\end{proof}

1291	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1292	[Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate]
1293	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1294	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1295	  % NOTE: tensor-order convention. The Lean uses Mathlib's `pullbackSpecIso`
1296	  % orientation `A ⊗[R] R'` (so the codomain is `(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` and the generator
1297	  % goes `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`); the prose below writes `R' ⊗_R A`. The two are the
1298	  % same module up to `TensorProduct.comm` — a faithful re-orientation, no content change.
1299	  \uses{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1300	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1301	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
1302	  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
1303	  % L933--938).
1304	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
1305	  % equality
1306	  % $$
1307	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
1308	  % $$
1309	  % as $R'$-modules."
1310	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
1311	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1312	  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be ring homomorphisms and \(M\) an
1313	  \(A\)-module. There is a bundled \(R'\)-linear isomorphism
1314	  \[
1315	    \operatorname{regroup} :
1316	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M ,
1317	  \]
1318	  the ``equality as \(R'\)-modules'' of the source, sending the generator
1319	  \((r' \otimes 1) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\); equivalently its inverse sends
1320	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). It carries \emph{no} flatness
1321	  hypothesis.
1322	\end{lemma}
1323	
1324	\begin{proof}
1325	  \uses{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1326	  The underlying \(R'\)-linear equivalence is supplied, already as a bundled
1327	  \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv}\) over \(R'\), by the natively \(R'\)-linear
1328	  pushout-cancellation equivalence of
1329	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}. Crucially, that helper bundles
1330	  \(R'\)-linearity \emph{internally}: there is no hand-written scalar-compatibility
1331	  obligation, no induction over elementary tensors, and in particular \emph{no} residual
1332	  \(r' \cdot 0 = 0\) bookkeeping branch. The only work left to this proof is therefore to
1333	  promote that \(R'\)-linear equivalence to a morphism of \(R'\)-module \emph{objects} in
1334	  the exact extension/restriction-of-scalars packaging that the codomain and domain reads
1335	  produce. We work in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation
1336	  (the same module as \(R' \otimes_R A\) up to the canonical commutativity isomorphism,
1337	  per the tensor-order convention note).
1338	
1339	  \emph{The lone residual obstruction is the \(A\)-action diamond, resolved at the object
1340	  level.} The pushout-cancellation helper takes its relative tensor product over the
1341	  \emph{canonical} \(A\)-algebra structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\)
1342	  (multiplication in the left factor), whereas the carrier object produced by the
1343	  dictionaries,
1344	  the extension of scalars of \(M\) along the left inclusion \(\iota : A \to A \otimes_R R'\),
1345	  tensors over the \(A\)-module structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\) induced by \(\iota\). These
1346	  two \(A\)-module structures on
1347	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) agree on elements but are distinct as data, so the two relative
1348	  tensor products are distinct types even though their underlying abelian groups and
1349	  their \(R'\)-actions coincide. This diamond is the \emph{only} thing the helper does
1350	  not already discharge, and — because the \(R'\)-linearity is now entirely internal to
1351	  the Mathlib equivalence — it is resolved purely at the carrier level by an
1352	  identity-on-elements \(R'\)-linear bridge
1353	  \[
1354	    eT : (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\operatorname{restr}} M
1355	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1356	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\mathrm{can}} M ,
1357	  \]
1358	  the identity map on generators, which is \(R'\)-linear because both sides carry the
1359	  same \(R'\)-action (restriction of scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\)).
1360	
1361	  Composing the bridge \(eT\) with the natively \(R'\)-linear helper equivalence and
1362	  promoting to an isomorphism of module objects yields the desired bundled
1363	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism of \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\) objects. Being a composite
1364	  of linear equivalences it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of its constituents
1365	  carries a flatness hypothesis.
1366	\end{proof}

1372	%       (3) lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose — the (g^* ⊣ g_*) transpose on sections.

1374	\begin{lemma}
1375	
1376	\leanok
1377	[The affine pullback--pushforward unit is the algebraic unit]
1378	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}
1379	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_unit_value}
1380	  \uses{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
1381	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma `lemma-affine-base-change'
1382	  % (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the "boils down to the equality" step
1383	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L932--937).
1384	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the equality
1385	  % $$ (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M $$ as $R'$-modules."
1386	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base change''
1387	  (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1388	  Let \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) be the canonical inclusion
1389	  (\(a \mapsto a \otimes 1\)). The unit of the
1390	  \(\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)^*, (\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_*\bigr)\)-adjunction
1391	  evaluated at the affine sheaf \(\widetilde M\), read on the global sections over
1392	  \(\operatorname{Spec} A\) through the tilde dictionaries
1393	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}, Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}) and
1394	  the tilde--\(\Gamma\) unit \(\widetilde{(-)}\Gamma\)-iso, equals the algebraic unit of the
1395	  extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\iota_A\), namely
1396	  \(\eta_M : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\).
1397	\end{lemma}
1398	\begin{proof}
1399	  \uses{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
1400	  This is the affine, square-free heart of the computation: there is no pullback square and
1401	  no base-change map yet, only the comparison of two units of two adjunctions related by the
1402	  two tilde dictionaries. The geometric adjunction
1403	  \(\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)^* \dashv (\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_*\bigr)\) on
1404	  sheaves of modules transports, under the tilde dictionaries which identify
1405	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)^*\) of a tilde with extension of scalars
1406	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}) and \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_*\) of a tilde
1407	  with restriction of scalars (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}), to the algebraic
1408	  extension/restriction adjunction \((\iota_A^{\,*} \dashv \iota_{A\,*})\) on
1409	  \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\). The dictionaries being the comparison isomorphisms of these
1410	  adjunctions, naturality identifies the two units; evaluating at \(M\) gives the stated
1411	  value \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\).
1412	\end{proof}

1615	  \label{lem:pullbackComp_inv_eq_leftAdjointCompIso_inv}
1616	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackComp_inv_eq_leftAdjointCompIso_inv}
1617	  \uses{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib, lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib,
1618	        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1619	  Let \(f : X \to Y\) and \(g : Y \to Z\) be composable morphisms of schemes. The inverse of the
1620	  project's pullback-composition pseudofunctor coherence
1621	  \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{f,g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) agrees with the inverse of the abstract
1622	  composite-adjunction comparison \(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}\)
1623	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}) built from the pushforward coherence
1624	  \(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f,g}\):
1625	  \[
1626	    (\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{f,g})^{\mathrm{inv}}
1627	      \;=\; \bigl(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f,g})\bigr)^{\mathrm{inv}}.
1628	  \]
1629	  \begin{proof}
1630	    \uses{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib, lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib,
1631	          lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1632	    Both sides have the same image under the conjugation (mate) bijection
1633	    \(\operatorname{conj}\bigl((g^{*}\dashv g_{*}).(f^{*}\dashv f_{*}),\ (f\circ g)^{*}\dashv (f\circ g)_{*}\bigr)\):
1634	    the left side is sent to \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f,g})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) by the project coherence
1635	    Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}, and the right side is sent to the same hom by
1636	    the Mathlib identity Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib}. Since conjugation
1637	    is injective, the two inverses are equal.
1638	  \end{proof}
1639	\end{lemma}
1640	
1641	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1642	[(conj-0$'$) Iso-level form: $\mathrm{pullbackComp}$ equals $\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}$]
1643	  \label{lem:pullbackComp_eq_leftAdjointCompIso}
1644	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackComp_eq_leftAdjointCompIso}
1645	  \uses{lem:pullbackComp_inv_eq_leftAdjointCompIso_inv}
1646	  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackComp_inv_eq_leftAdjointCompIso_inv}, the inverse-level
1647	  identity upgrades to a full isomorphism equality
1648	  \[

content:
# Strategy

## Goal

Close the `sorry`-bearing nodes of the **Čech-independent leg** of the parent's
`thm:fga_pic_representability` cone (Kleiman FGA, "The Picard scheme", §4), then merge back:

- **FBC** — `lem:affine_base_change_pushforward` + `thm:flat_base_change_pushforward`
  (the i=0 base-change map `g^* f_* F ⟶ f'_* g'^* F` is an isomorphism).
- **GF** — `thm:generic_flatness` with algebraic core `thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`.
- **QUOT** — `def:hilbert_polynomial`, `def:quot_functor`, `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
  `thm:grassmannian_representable`.

End-state: zero project `sorry` in the 29-node closure, zero project axioms, kernel-only axioms.
Names/labels are the parent's so finished work merges back into its A.2.c engine.

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| FBC-A1 — affine iso (mate `gstar_transpose`) | PIVOT (blueprint+scaffold) | 3–6 | ~150–350 | `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` (DONE); affine tilde-transport bypass | **Conjugate route EXHAUSTED in-loop** (5 iters 037–041: the multi-layer composite-adjunction recognition is a bespoke Mathlib-absent construction the prover could not assemble). Per the armed protocol NO further conjugate/analogist rounds. **New route = affine tilde-transport**: establish IsIso of the canonical `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` at the affine-local level via the proven tilde-iso, bypassing the section-level `gstar_transpose` coherence. iter-042 authors the blueprint section; scaffold + prove follow. |
| FBC-A2 — affine/locality reduction (`@2348`) | ACTIVE (parallelisable) | 3–6 | ~200–500 | locality-of-`IsIso` for `SheafOfModules` on a basis (Mathlib-absent) | Independent of A1; locality criterion + `base_change_map_affine_local` blueprint section EXIST. Consult on basis-locality then prover. Can open in parallel with the A1 tilde-transport build. |
| FBC-B — globalization, H⁰-equalizer | ACTIVE (parallel) | 2–5 | ~100–260 | `tensorEqLocusEquiv`; sheaf fork for `SheafOfModules` | eqLocus sub-lane + `baseChangeGammaEquiv` payoff DONE (axiom-clean). `FlatBaseChangeGlobal.lean` exists but UNWIRED (not imported by root) + frontier decls are TODO placeholders → needs scaffold. Chain assembly gated on FBC-A's affine iso. |
| GF-geo — `genericFlatness` | ACTIVE (UNBLOCKED by gap1) | 2–4 | ~120–300 | G1-core (now buildable); `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_maximal` | base `[IsIntegral S]`+`[QuasiCompact p]` required. G1 bottomed at gap1 (now CLOSED) — GF-G1 = direct application of G1-core; openable once G1-core lands + importable. G3 (per-patch freeness ⟹ flatness) gap1-independent but needs blueprint+scaffold + `gf_torsion_reindex` OreLocalization fix. |
| QUOT-defs consumers — G1-core / gap2 / annihilator / P2 | ACTIVE (gap1 unblocked) | 2–4 | ~120–280 | gap1 (DONE); affine-cover reduction; `SheafOfModules.free`/`pullback` | gap1 CLOSED ⟹ **G1-core** `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent` (Spec R, 2-line corollary) buildable now; then **gap2** (general scheme X via affine cover) → annihilator forward inclusion. P2 (rank-`r` local-freeness predicate) gap1-independent, small. All in QuotScheme.lean; 4 iter-176 stubs protected. |
| SNAP-S1/S3 — section-module input + `Φ_s` extraction | BLOCKED | 3–6 | ~150–400 | `SheafOfModules` tensor powers of `L_s`; `existsUnique_hilbertPoly` | GATED on Open Q1 + `def:sectionGradedRing` (tensor-powers sub-build) |
| QUOT-repr — `thm:grassmannian_representable` | BLOCKED | 6–12 | ~400–1000+ | Grassmannian-of-quotients as a scheme; RelativeSpec → `RepresentableBy` | deepest target; GR-cells/glue/sep DONE; quot/repr in Routes |

## Completed

| Phase | Iters (done@ · used) | LOC | Files | Key results | Reusable techniques | Pitfalls |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| GF-alg — algebraic core | 022 · ~9 | ~900 | FlatteningStratification.lean | `genericFlatnessAlgebraic` axiom-clean (Nitsure §4 dévissage); L4/L5 axiom-clean | `g:=g0·g1` + `IsIntegral.exists_multiple_integral_of_isLocalization`; ring↔module bridge `IsLocalizedModule.iso` + `LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization`; strong induction generalizing base domain | hand-built `Algebra A Cg` via `letI` is an `isDefEq` dead end (use ambient instances); deep stack needs `maxHeartbeats 1600000` |
| FBC RegroupHelper | 011 · 4 | ~120 | Cohomology_RegroupHelper | `regroupEquiv` `(A⊗_R R')⊗_A M ≅ R'⊗_R M` axiom-clean | `eT` identity-bridge + `TensorProduct.induction_on` to beat transparent-instance diamonds | element `map_smul'` zero-branch needed `erw [TensorProduct.zero_tmul]` |
| GR-cells (charts+cocycle) | 012 · 2 | ~600 | GrassmannianCells.lean | big-cell charts, transition maps, `lem:gr_cocycle` — 28 decls | `IsLocalization.Away.lift` for localized transition maps; prove distributed-matrix forms by `exact` then `rw` | `rw [Matrix.map_mul]` fails on Away-base-changed matrices (hidden `algebraMap` diamond) |
| GR-glue + separated | 034 · 4 | ~350 | GrassmannianCells.lean | `Grassmannian.scheme` (GlueData), `isSeparated`/`isSeparatedToSpecZ`, `diagonalRingMap_surjective` | `Spec ℤ` genuinely terminal for `Scheme.{0}` ⟹ `toSpecZ` + glue by `IsTerminal.hom_ext`; faithful `Proj.isSeparated` port (per-patch closed immersion) | `convert!`/`pullback.map_fst` absent (use `convert … using 1`, `pullback.lift_fst`); `← Spec.map_comp` bare `rw` dead on the Scheme-cat diamond (route via `show`) |
| SNAP-S2 power-series engine | 012 · 1 | ~180 | QuotScheme.lean (`IsRatHilb`) | antidifference + `IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq` (power-series half of Stacks 00K1), 8 decls | telescoping via `invOneSubPow`; `IsRatHilb` predicate isolates series bookkeeping | `PowerSeries.C` ring arg implicit; `open … in` must precede docstring |
| SNAP-S2 graded Hilbert–Serre rationality | 020 · 9 | ~1290 | GradedHilbertSerre.lean | keystone `gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational` (Stacks 00K1) axiom-clean | Route-2 ambient-subquotient pairs (`Naux⊓ℳn`) sidestep quotient-carrier gradings | bundled `DirectSum.Decomposition`/`IsInternal` over quotient/subtype carrier is a hard `isDefEq` dead end (Route 2 supersedes) |
| QUOT-defs P1 predicates | 011 · 1 | ~90 | QuotScheme.lean | schematic-support, proper-support via `IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`/`.subscheme` | annihilator-ideal-sheaf needs NO QCoh bridge | — |
| GR-proper — valuative criterion | 038 · ~5 | ~300 | GrassmannianCells.lean | `Grassmannian.isProper` (Gr(d,r) proper over ℤ); E4/E5/existence-lift | Nitsure §1 DVR-filler (minimal minor J, factor through R⊂K); term-mode glue for Spec.map composition | keyed `rw` dead on Scheme-cat diamond (use `congrArg`/`calc`); `existence_lift` noncomputable; pass `(R:=S.R)` to E2/E3 |
| QUOT gap1 — section-loc descent | 041 · ~14 | ~600 | QuotScheme.lean | gap1 `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent` + keystone `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` + `Hfr` producer (Hartshorne II.5.3 / Stacks invert-f-sections), WITHOUT global QCoh≃Mod | keep composite immersion `j` OPAQUE in helpers; σ `S`-vs-`Γ(Spec S,⊤)` rebasing is definitional (`rfl`) | concrete triple-composite `j` → >3.2M-heartbeat `whnf` runaway in form-coercion; general-U `_of_cover` `Hfr` is an unprovable trap (use basic-open form) |

## Routes

Single route per target; the leg is a fan of independent leaves merging back upstream. FBC-A and
GF-geo are the live frontier; QUOT is authorable in parallel (all four files import only Mathlib).
FBC-B follows FBC-A; QUOT-repr follows QUOT-defs, SNAP, and the RelativeSpec strengthening.

**FBC route.** The affine lemma `g^* f_* F ≅ f'_* g'^* F` (Stacks 02KH part 2) reduces at the MODULE
level to the regroup iso `R' ⊗_R M ≅ R' ⊗_R A ⊗_A M` (`regroupEquiv`, DONE, axiom-clean). Iso-ness of
the canonical map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is **already free** (`conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR` of
`gammaPushforwardNatIso`, = `pullback_spec_tilde_iso`); the sole open work is the **coherence**
`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs` / `gstar_transpose` (the canonical map's name is parent-frozen, so
the coherence is genuinely owed, not dissolvable by redefining the map). The affine close has two
obligations: **(2) the section-level identity** (the mate coherence) and **(1) the affine reduction**
(identify `(pushforwardBaseChangeMap …).app U` with the affine–affine model over the restricted square;
restriction-compatibility, Mathlib-absent). Obligation 2 (the `gstar_transpose` mate coherence) had been
pursued via the **conjugate-counit calculus**, now EXHAUSTED in-loop:
- *(CONJUGATE ROUTE EXHAUSTED — 5 iters 037–041.)* All three closing legs of `_legs_conj` are axiom-clean
  (conj-2b `pullbackLeg`, conj-2c `pushforwardCollapse`, conj-2d `crossLayer`) and iter-041 added a
  verified in-proof Γ-collapse stage, but the HEAVY crux — recognising the cross-layer composite as a
  single `conjugateEquiv` value spanning all five adjunction layers — is a bespoke, Mathlib-absent
  construction that neither the one-shot reframing (iters 037–039) nor the layer-by-layer Fallback B
  (iters 040–041) could assemble. Per the armed protocol: **NO further conjugate or analogist rounds on
  `_legs_conj`.** The proven conj scaffold (conj-1a/1b/2b/2c/2d + Γ-collapse) stays as the dictionary for
  whatever route consumes it.
- *(PIVOT — affine tilde-transport.)* The chosen next route abandons the section-level `gstar_transpose`
  identity and instead establishes **IsIso of the canonical `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` directly at the
  affine-local level** via the already-proven `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` (Stacks 01I9) + `regroupEquiv`
  (DONE), feeding the affine-open locality criterion (`base_change_map_affine_local`, blueprint present).
  This is structurally different from the conjugate calculus: it proves the IsIso the downstream
  equalizer globalization consumes, rather than the parent-frozen map's exact section value. iter-042
  authors the blueprint section; scaffold + prover follow. Element-`ext` (iter-035 dead end) stays DROPPED.

Globalize **Čech-cohomology-free**: `H⁰(X,F)` is the equalizer `∏Γ(Uᵢ,F) ⇉ ∏Γ(Uᵢⱼ,F)`, and flat `−⊗B`
preserves that finite equalizer (`tensorEqLocusEquiv`). (Excluded direct-on-sections / term-mode
encodings: see iter sidecars.)

**GF route.** Algebraic core `genericFlatnessAlgebraic` DONE (Nitsure §4 induction, base-domain
generalization). Geometric `genericFlatness` (`[IsQuasicoherent]`+`[IsFiniteType]`) wraps it: pass to a
non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` (A noetherian domain), cover `p⁻¹(Spec A)` by finitely many affine
`W_j = Spec B_j` (finite-type over A), read `M_j = Γ(F,W_j)` finite over `B_j`, apply the algebraic form
per patch for `f_j ≠ 0`, take `V = D(∏ f_j)`, conclude flatness from freeness over the localisation. G1
(finite-section module on affine) bottoms at gap1.

**QUOT route.** Foundational decisions:
- *Hilbert-polynomial encoding = graded Hilbert function.* `def:hilbert_polynomial` = the polynomial
  `Φ_s` agreeing for `m≫0` with `m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^m)` of a f.g. graded module
  (Hartshorne I.7.5; Nitsure §1). Routes through `Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly` (`[CharZero]`) +
  the graded Hilbert–Serre rationality `lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational` (DONE, axiom-clean). Same
  polynomial as the cohomological χ without higher cohomology.
- *Rationality engine = Route 2* (Stacks 00K1 over pairs `N'≤N` of submodules of a FIXED ambient graded
  κ-module — avoids the quotient-carrier `isDefEq` pathology). DONE.
- *SNAP-S1 input* (GATED — Open Q1). Primary route: chosen f.g. presentation — every coherent `F` on
  `Proj S` is `M̃` for a f.g. graded `M` (f.g. by construction), sidestepping the doubtful "Γ_*(F) f.g."
  lemma. Canonicity of `Φ_s` may need a Serre `m≫0` agreement; resolve route before any S1 prover.
- *QUOT-defs predicate sub-builds*: P1 schematic-support + `IsProper`-support DONE; P2 rank-`r`
  local-freeness for `SheafOfModules` NEXT. `Grassmannian := QuotFunctor (𝟙 S) V Φ_d`. Forward annihilator
  char. is bridge-gated on gap1 (local/stalk shortcuts do NOT avoid it). gap1 route C→P1[DONE]→D→assembly
  is in the phase table + Mathlib gaps below.
- *QUOT-repr decomposed*: GR-cells/glue/separated (DONE); GR-proper (valuative criterion, ACTIVE); GR-quot
  (tautological rank-`d` quotient + universal property); GR-repr (functor-of-points ⟹ `RepresentableBy`,
  needs `thm:relative_spec_univ` strengthened via `representableByEquiv`).

## Open strategic questions

- **Q1 — SNAP route DECISION (blocks S1; pick one, then sub-build).** Canonicity of `Φ_s` across graded
  presentations needs either (a) chosen-presentation `Φ_s` + a cited Serre `m≫0` agreement, or (b) an
  H¹-free finiteness with an exact reference. Decision: defer the pick until gap1 lands (gap1 unblocks
  GF-G1 + the annihilator, higher leverage); trigger = once gap1 closes, dispatch a reference-retriever
  for the Serre `m≫0` agreement (the "Hartshorne II.5.17" attribution is unverified/likely wrong — read
  before committing), and decide (a) vs (b). The `def:sectionGradedRing` tensor-powers sub-build is a
  separate Mathlib-gradient prerequisite owed regardless.
- **Q2 — FBC `gstar_transpose` discharge — RESOLVED (iter-042): conjugate route exhausted, PIVOT to
  affine tilde-transport.** The conjugate-component calculus is exhausted in-loop (5 iters, 037–041:
  one-shot reframing then layer-by-layer Fallback B both failed to assemble the multi-layer composite
  `adjL`/`adjR`). Per the armed protocol there are NO further conjugate/analogist rounds. New route: prove
  IsIso of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` at the affine-local level via `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` + the
  locality criterion, bypassing the section-level coherence (Routes §FBC). Reversal signal: if the
  tilde-transport blueprint surfaces a structural obstruction (the canonical map's IsIso genuinely cannot
  be read off the tilde-iso without the section identity), escalate to the user — the conjugate route is
  already closed off. Obligation 1 (A2 affine reduction) is a separate owed build, unaffected.
- **Q3 — `def:hilbert_polynomial` standard-graded hypothesis (fence before any SNAP/S1 prover).** The
  Stacks-00K1 rationality engine requires `S₊` generated in degree 1, so the Hilbert-polynomial definition
  must carry a standard-graded / very-ample hypothesis (ample-only is insufficient). Verify the current
  Lean signature of `def:hilbert_polynomial` and the SNAP route statements carry it before spending prover
  budget on S1.
- **Merge-back signature check.** Confirm the re-signed `genericFlatness`/QUOT decls
  (`[IsQuasicoherent]`+`[IsFiniteType]`) match the parent cone's expected signatures.
- **Shared qcoh-affine-local infra (GF-G1 ∩ QUOT).** Both bottom at the same `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`
  ≡ gap1 node, now decomposed into (C) `overRestrictIso` + (D) section-localization descent (the latter is
  the shared reusable keystone). Decision: build it ONCE QUOT-side (its `Scheme.Modules.*`
  namespace + blueprint home + a live consumer), NOT a copy in FlatteningStratification. Once gap1 lands,
  a mathlib-analogist API-alignment pass gates shared-extraction vs adding the import.

## Mathlib gaps & new material

Gaps to fill (detail in Routes):
- FBC-A: the `_legs`/`gstar_transpose` mate coherence (conjugate-side discharge; or bypass via the
  affine-local explicit-inverse route). No new Mathlib lemma needed.
- FBC-B: H⁰-as-equalizer / finite-affine-cover sheaf-condition packaging for `SheafOfModules` (DONE).
- GF-geo: **G1** (qcoh+finite-type ⟹ `Γ(F,W)` finite on affine `W`, via gap1) and **G3** (flat-locality
  assembly: per-patch freeness on a finite cover ⟹ flatness over `Γ(S,U)`).
- **gap1 — QCoh≃Mod affine descent (keystone bottom):** `IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` on
  `Spec R`. Mathlib has `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff` + the global-`Presentation` case only. The in-file iff
  engine + finite-cover front DONE. Two genuinely-missing lemmas remain
  (`analogies/quot-gap1-transport.md`): **(C) `overRestrictIso`** — the `M.over U ↔ M.restrict U.ι /
  Spec R_r` bridge (the ONE lemma touching the slice site; `backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`
  tames the synthInstance timeout); **(D) section-localization descent** (Stacks `lemma-invert-f-sections`
  / Hartshorne II.5.3) — the real keystone (sheaf equalizer + `IsLocalization.flat` over the finite
  cover). P1 (per-affine local-tilde) DONE iter-034. NOTE:
  obstacle "no restriction functor" was FALSE — `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor`/`pullback` exist.
- **SheafOfModules tensor powers (blocks `def:sectionGradedRing` ⟹ SNAP):** `L_s^{⊗m}` + lax-monoidal `Γ`
  absent; Mathlib-gradient sub-build owed before SNAP.
- QUOT predicates: rank-`r` local-freeness for `SheafOfModules`. QUOT-repr: Grassmannian-of-quotients as
  a scheme (Nitsure §1/§5 big-cell patching).

New project material:
- `genericFlatnessAlgebraic` (done), re-signed `genericFlatness` (+`IsQuasicoherent`+`IsFiniteType`).
- Coherence encoding = `[IsQuasicoherent]` + `[IsFiniteType]` over the locally noetherian base.
- QUOT defs with tightened signatures; `Grassmannian` via `QuotFunctor`; representability as `IsRepresentable`.

Now I have a good picture of STRATEGY.md. Let me continue reading the QuotScheme.tex chapter for the gap1 and G1-core material, and read more of FlatBaseChange.tex to understand the tilde-transport status.

Let me now:
1. Read the QuotScheme.tex focusing on gap1/G1-core sections
2. Read more of FlatBaseChange.tex (the crucial section)
3. Run leandag
4. Run archon blueprint-doctor
Now let me read the QuotScheme chapter for gap1/G1-core, and the FBC chapter for the key later sections.

1663	\begin{lemma}
1664	
1665	\leanok
1666	[Pseudofunctoriality of the pullback--pushforward unit]
1667	  \label{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
1668	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
1669	  \uses{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib, lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib,
1670	        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1671	  Let \(a : X_1 \to X_2\) and \(b : X_2 \to X_3\) be composable morphisms of schemes and let
1672	  \(N\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_{X_3}\)-module. Write \(\eta^{h}\) for the unit of the
1673	  pullback--pushforward adjunction \((h^{*}\dashv h_{*})\) attached to a morphism \(h\), and
1674	  \(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b}\), \(\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b}\) for the pseudofunctor
1675	  composition coherences. Then
1676	  \[
1677	    \eta^{b}_{N} \;\circ\; b_{*}\bigl(\eta^{a}_{\,b^{*}N}\bigr)
1678	      \;\circ\; (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}_{N}
1679	    \;=\;
1680	    \eta^{a\circ b}_{N} \;\circ\;
1681	      (a\circ b)_{*}\bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}_{N}\bigr),
1682	  \]
1683	  where composites are read left to right. In words: the unit of the composite morphism
1684	  \(a \circ b\), reindexed by the inverse pullback coherence, factors as the \(b\)-unit, followed
1685	  by \(b_{*}\) of the \(a\)-unit, followed by the pushforward coherence. This is the leg-reindex
1686	  engine consumed by Seam~2 (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}): with \(a\) an
1687	  isomorphism and \(b\) an affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-map it turns the generic projection-leg
1688	  unit into the affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-unit modulo the transparent coherences.
1689	\end{lemma}
1690	\begin{proof}
1691	  \uses{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib, lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib,
1692	        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1693	  Apply the unit-across-conjugate coherence Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib} to the
1694	  composite adjunction \((b^{*}\dashv b_{*}) . (a^{*}\dashv a_{*})\), the adjunction
1695	  \(\bigl((a\circ b)^{*}\dashv (a\circ b)_{*}\bigr)\), and the left-adjoint transformation
1696	  \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}\). This yields an identity whose right-hand factor
1697	  is the conjugate (mate) of \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}\); by
1698	  Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib} that mate is exactly the pushforward
1699	  coherence \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}\). Expanding the unit of the composite
1700	  adjunction by Lemma~\ref{lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib} —
1701	  \(\bigl((b^{*}\dashv b_{*}).(a^{*}\dashv a_{*})\bigr)\text{-unit}_N
1702	    = \eta^{b}_N \circ b_{*}(\eta^{a}_{b^{*}N})\) — and reassociating the resulting composite gives
1703	  the stated equation.
1704	\end{proof}

1754	\begin{lemma}
1755	
1756	\leanok
1757	[$\Gamma$-collapse of the pushforward-congruence coherence]
1758	  \label{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom}
1759	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom}
1760	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
1761	  Let \(f, g : X \to \operatorname{Spec} R\) be morphisms of schemes with \(f = g\), and \(M\)
1762	  an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Applying the global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) to the component
1763	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,(f = g))^{\mathrm{hom}}_M\) of the congruence coherence yields the
1764	  canonical \(\operatorname{eqToHom}\) transport along the object equality induced by \(f = g\):
1765	  \[
1766	    \Gamma\bigl((\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,(f = g))^{\mathrm{hom}}_M\bigr)
1767	      = \operatorname{eqToHom}(h),
1768	  \]
1769	  where \(h\) is the equality of the two section modules forced by \(f = g\). This is the precise
1770	  form: the coherence is not literally the identity but the canonical transport across the equality
1771	  of domains, which is the identity only after \(f\) and \(g\) have been made definitionally equal.

1778	  \(\operatorname{eqToHom}\) transport along the induced equality of section modules. It carries no
1779	  substantive content beyond this transport.

1937	\begin{lemma}[Conjugation distributes over composition (reassoc simp lemma)]
1938	  \label{lem:conjugateEquiv_comp_mathlib}
1939	  \lean{CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv_comp}
1940	  \mathlibok
1941	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1942	  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : L_1 \dashv R_1\), \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : L_2 \dashv R_2\),
1943	  \(\mathrm{adj}_3 : L_3 \dashv R_3\) be adjunctions between the same pair of categories, and let
1944	  \(\alpha : L_2 \Rightarrow L_1\), \(\beta : L_3 \Rightarrow L_2\) be transformations of left adjoints.
1945	  Then the conjugates compose, with order reversed onto the right-adjoint side:
1946	  \[
1947	    \operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_2)\,\alpha \;\circ\;
1948	    \operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_2,\mathrm{adj}_3)\,\beta
1949	      \;=\; \operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_3)\,(\beta \circ \alpha) .
1950	  \]

3527	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3528	  Let \(A \to B\) be a flat ring homomorphism (more generally, let \(B\) be a flat
3529	  \(A\)-module that is also an \(A\)-algebra, with the scalar-tower compatibility),
3530	  and let \(f, g : N \rightrightarrows P\) be a pair of \(A\)-linear maps. Write
3531	  \(\operatorname{eq}(f, g) = \{x \in N : f(x) = g(x)\}\) for their equalizer. Then the
3532	  canonical \(B\)-linear comparison map
3533	  \[
3534	    B \otimes_A \operatorname{eq}(f, g)
3535	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
3536	    \operatorname{eq}\bigl(\mathbf{1}_B \otimes f,\ \mathbf{1}_B \otimes g\bigr)
3537	  \]
3538	  is an isomorphism; that is, base change along a flat algebra commutes with the
3539	  formation of the finite equalizer (equivalently, of the kernel: the case
3540	  \(g = 0\) is \texttt{LinearMap.tensorKerEquiv}, underlain by
3541	  \texttt{Module.Flat.ker\_lTensor\_eq} and \texttt{Module.Flat.eqLocus\_lTensor\_eq}).
3542	  Since \(- \otimes_A B\) also preserves finite products, it follows that
3543	  \(- \otimes_A B\) carries the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) equalizer
3544	  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \rightrightarrows
3545	    \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})\) of a finite cover to the corresponding
3546	  equalizer of the base-changed cover.
3547	\end{lemma}
3548	
3549	\begin{lemma}[Sheaf condition as an equalizer of products]
3550	  \label{lem:sheaf_equalizer_products_mathlib}
3551	  \lean{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheafEqualizerProducts}
3552	  \mathlibok
3553	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3554	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a presheaf with values in a category with products on a
3555	  topological space, and let \(\mathcal{U} = \{U_i\}_{i \in \iota}\) be a family of
3556	  opens with union \(U = \bigcup_i U_i\). Then \(\mathcal{F}\) satisfies the sheaf
3557	  condition on \(\mathcal{U}\) if and only if the fork
3558	  \[
3559	    \mathcal{F}(U)
3560	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\mathrm{res}\;}\;
3561	    \prod_i \mathcal{F}(U_i)
3562	    \;\underset{\mathrm{rightRes}}{\overset{\mathrm{leftRes}}{\rightrightarrows}}\;
3563	    \prod_{(i,j)} \mathcal{F}(U_i \cap U_j)
3564	  \]
3565	  is a limit (an equalizer), where the two parallel maps restrict the \(U_i\)- and
3566	  \(U_j\)-components to \(U_i \cap U_j\). This is Mathlib's
3567	  \texttt{isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafEqualizerProducts} together with the fork
3568	  \texttt{SheafConditionEqualizerProducts.fork}; it is exactly Čech degree
3569	  \(0 \to 1\) and requires no separatedness.
3570	\end{lemma}
3571	
3572	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3573	[Finite affine cover with quasi-compact overlaps]
3574	  \label{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}
3575	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact}
3576	  A quasi-compact scheme \(X\) admits a \emph{finite} affine open cover
3577	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\). If moreover \(X\) is
3578	  quasi-separated, then each pairwise intersection \(U_{ij} = U_i \cap U_j\) is
3579	  quasi-compact; and if \(X\) is separated, each \(U_{ij}\) is in fact affine.
3580	  \begin{proof}
3581	    The affine opens form a basis, so \(X\) is covered by affine opens; quasi-compactness
3582	    extracts a finite subcover \(U_1, \dots, U_t\) (by Mathlib's affine cover and the
3583	    finite-subcover property of a quasi-compact space). Quasi-separatedness says the
3584	    intersection of two quasi-compact opens is quasi-compact, so each \(U_{ij}\) is
3585	    quasi-compact. When \(X\) is separated the diagonal is a closed immersion, whence
3586	    the intersection of two affine opens is affine (Mathlib, for separated schemes).
3587	  \end{proof}
3588	\end{lemma}

3597	  % application; the consolidated existential `Modules.exists_finite_affineCover_isLimit_sheafConditionFork`
3598	  % now has its own block, Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover}.
3599	  \uses{lem:sheaf_equalizer_products_mathlib, lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}

946	  exist a triangular \(A\)-algebra automorphism \(e\) of \(A[X_0, \dots, X_n]\) and a

953	\begin{proof}

955	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (support-dimension drop, Nagata normalisation).}
956	  Apply the Nagata substitution \(X_i \mapsto X_i + X_0^{r^{\,i}}\) for \(i = 1, \dots, n\)
957	  and \(X_0 \mapsto X_0\), with \(r\) larger than every exponent occurring in \(F\): it is a
958	  triangular (hence invertible) \(A\)-algebra automorphism \(e\) of \(A[X_0, \dots, X_n]\).
959	  Under this substitution the distinct monomials of \(F\) acquire distinct degrees in
960	  \(X_0\), so \(e(F)\), expanded as a polynomial in \(X_0\), has a unique top-degree term
961	  whose coefficient is the coefficient \(c \in A\) of the top total-degree monomial of
962	  \(F\); since \(F \ne 0\) this \(c \ne 0\). Put \(g := c\). The degree of \(e(F)\) in
963	  \(X_0\) equals the total degree of \(F\), and its leading coefficient in \(X_0\) is the
964	  constant \(g\). Passing to \(A_g = A[g^{-1}]\), the image of \(g\) is a unit, so the
965	  image of \(e(F)\) in \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_n]\) is monic in \(X_0\) up to the unit \(g\).

1107	  \(g \in A\) such that the image of \(e(F)\) in \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]\) has a unit
1108	  leading coefficient in \(X_0\). The automorphism \(e\) induces a ring isomorphism
1109	  \(P_d/(F) \cong P_d/(e\,F)\), so \(T\) is also finite over \(P_d/(e\,F)\). Localising at
1110	  the powers of \(g\) (localisation commutes with the quotient), \(T_g\) is finite over
1111	  \(P_d[g^{-1}]/(e\,F) = A_g[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]/(e\,F)\).
1112	
1113	  \emph{Elimination.} Apply the shared engine
1114	  \cref{lem:gf_mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_monic} with \(R = A_g\) and \(p\) the image of
1115	  \(e(F)\) (whose leading coefficient in \(X_0\) is a unit): the quotient
1116	  \(A_g[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]/(e\,F)\) is module-finite over the coefficient subring
1117	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{d-1}]\), a polynomial ring in \(m' := d - 1\) variables over
1118	  \(A_g\).
1119	
1120	  \emph{Localisation transport.} The elimination step records module-finiteness of the
1121	  \emph{quotient ring} over its coefficient subring, but the conclusion of the lemma is
1122	  about a localisation of the torsion module \(T\). Making the two agree is the
1123	  mathematical content of this step, which proceeds as follows.
1124	
1125	  Two distinct localisations of \(T\) are in play and must be identified. The natural
1126	  setting for the elimination is the localisation \(T'_g := T[\,\mathcal{C}^{-1}]\) of \(T\)
1127	  at the multiplicative set \(\mathcal{C} = \{\,c \cdot g^{\,k} : k \ge 0\,\}\) of constant
1128	  polynomials \(g^k\) inside \(P_d\); this localisation carries, for free, the full
1129	  module structure over the localised polynomial ring
1130	  \(P_d[g^{-1}] = A_g[X_0, \dots, X_{d-1}]\), its finiteness, and the quotient structure by
1131	  \((e\,F)\). The \emph{goal}, however, is phrased with \(T_g := T[g^{-1}]\), the
1132	  localisation of \(T\) at the powers of the single element \(g \in A\). One first proves
1133	  \(T'_g\) module-finite over \(R := A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\) and then transports that
1134	  finiteness to \(T_g\).
1135	
1136	  The change of variables \(e\) is transported to the localised polynomial ring as a
1137	  \emph{ring isomorphism} \(\bar e : P_d[g^{-1}] \xrightarrow{\sim} P_d[g^{-1}]\), obtained
1138	  by localising \(e\) at \(\mathcal{C}\) (which \(e\) preserves, since \(e\) fixes the
1139	  constants \(c\,X^0\)); it carries the localised annihilator \(F_g\) to the
1140	  Nagata-normalised generator \(G\) and fixes the constants coming from the base. Passing
1141	  to quotients, \(\bar e\) descends to a ring isomorphism
1142	  \(\psi : P_d[g^{-1}]/(F_g) \xrightarrow{\sim} P_d[g^{-1}]/(G)\) of the two quotient rings
1143	  (using that the image of the ideal \((F_g)\) under \(\bar e\) is \((G)\)). Both quotient
1144	  rings act on \(T'_g\), and module-finiteness over \(R\) of the elimination-side quotient
1145	  \(P_d[g^{-1}]/(G)\) is transported across \(\psi\) — a ring isomorphism of the
1146	  \emph{acting} ring compatible with the fixed \(R\)-algebra structure — by
1147	  \cref{lem:gf_finite_of_quotient_ringequiv}, yielding \(T'_g\) module-finite over \(R\).
1148	
1149	  Finally the two localisations are identified. Restricting scalars of the canonical
1150	  \(P_d\)-localisation map of \(T\) along \(A \to P_d\) exhibits \(T'_g\) as a localisation
1151	  of \(T\) \emph{at the powers of \(g\) over \(A\)} — this descent of the
1152	  localised-module property along the scalar tower is
1153	  \cref{lem:gf_islocalizedmodule_restrictscalars} — so the universal property produces a
1154	  linear isomorphism \(T_g \cong T'_g\) identifying the two.
1155	
1156	  \emph{Action-diamond subtlety.} The conclusion of the lemma requires an
1157	  \(A_g\)-module structure on \(T_g\) sitting in a scalar tower
1158	  \(A_g \to A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}] \to T_g\). One cannot simply install an anonymous
1159	  \(A_g\)-action by fiat: \(T_g = T[g^{-1}]\) already carries its \emph{canonical}
1160	  localisation action of \(A_g\), and this canonical action is the one the scalar-tower
1161	  requirement sees. Hence the identification \(T_g \cong T'_g\) must be promoted to an
1162	  \(A_g\)-linear isomorphism — which it is, because the transported action agrees with the
1163	  canonical localisation action on the \(A_g\)-constants: the constant
1164	  \(\mathcal{C}\)-action of \(A_g\) on \(T'_g\) factors through the original \(A\)-action on
1165	  \(T\) exactly as the canonical \(g\)-localisation action does. With the isomorphism made
1166	  \(A_g\)-linear, the \(R\)-module structure and finiteness pull back from \(T'_g\) to
1167	  \(T_g\) by \cref{lem:gf_pullback_module_transport}, and the scalar tower
1168	  \(A_g \to R \to T_g\) follows.
1169	
1170	  \emph{Transitivity.} Composing the two module-finite extensions, \(T_g\) is finite over
1171	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\). Since \(m' = d - 1 < d\) (using \(d \ge 1\)), this is the
1172	  required reindexing; no Krull-dimension theory is needed, the number of variables
1173	  dropping by exactly one through the single-variable elimination.
1174	\end{proof}
1175	
1176	\subsubsection{Module-structure transport helpers (reindex)}
1177	\label{sec:gf_reindex_transport_helpers}
1178	
1179	The localisation-transport step of \cref{lem:gf_torsion_reindex} moves a module structure
1180	and its finiteness across (i) an additive equivalence of the underlying groups, (ii) a ring
1181	isomorphism of the acting ring, and (iii) a change of localising monoid along a scalar
1182	tower. The following helper lemmas package those three transport principles. They are general
1183	module-theory facts; no external source is cited.
1184	
1185	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1186	[Module transport along an additive equivalence]
1187	  \label{lem:gf_pullback_module_transport}
1188	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.pullbackModuleAddEquiv}
1189	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.finite_of_pullbackModuleAddEquiv}
1190	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.pullback_isScalarTower}
1191	  Let \(e : M \xrightarrow{\sim} N\) be an additive equivalence and let \(M\) carry an
1192	  \(R\)-module structure. Pulling the action back along \(e\) by
1193	  \(r \cdot y := e\big(r \cdot e^{-1}(y)\big)\) makes \(N\) an \(R\)-module for which \(e\)
1194	  is \(R\)-linear. Under this transported structure: (a) if \(M\) is module-finite over
1195	  \(R\) then so is \(N\); and (b) if \(M\) additionally carries a compatible action of a
1196	  base ring \(A_g\) with scalar tower \(A_g \to R \to M\), then the pulled-back actions on
1197	  \(N\) again form a scalar tower \(A_g \to R \to N\).
1198	\end{lemma}
1199	\begin{proof}
1200	  The module axioms for \(y \mapsto e(r \cdot e^{-1}(y))\) follow termwise from those on
1201	  \(M\) together with the fact that \(e\) and \(e^{-1}\) are additive and mutually inverse;
1202	  by construction \(e\) is then an \(R\)-linear isomorphism. Statement (a) follows by
1203	  transporting module-finiteness across the \(R\)-linear isomorphism \(e\).
1204	  Statement (b) checks the scalar-tower identity
1205	  \((a\,r)\cdot y = a\cdot(r\cdot y)\) on \(N\) by transporting it across \(e\) and applying
1206	  associativity of the scalar action on \(M\).
1207	\end{proof}
1208	
1209	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1210	[Finite transport along a ring isomorphism of the acting ring]
1211	  \label{lem:gf_finite_of_quotient_ringequiv}
1212	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.finite_of_quotientRingEquiv}
1213	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring and let \(\psi : B_1 \xrightarrow{\sim} B_2\) be a ring
1214	  isomorphism between two \(R\)-algebras that is compatible with the structure maps, i.e.\
1215	  \(\psi \circ (\text{algebra map } R \to B_1) = (\text{algebra map } R \to B_2)\). If
1216	  \(B_2\) is module-finite over \(R\) and \(M\) is module-finite over \(B_1\) (with the
1217	  scalar tower \(R \to B_1 \to M\)), then \(M\) is module-finite over \(R\).
1218	\end{lemma}
1219	\begin{proof}
1220	  Compatibility of \(\psi^{-1}\) with the structure maps follows from that of \(\psi\), so
1221	  \(\psi^{-1}\) is an \(R\)-algebra isomorphism; transporting \(R\)-finiteness of \(B_2\)
1222	  across it makes \(B_1\) module-finite over \(R\). Transitivity of module-finiteness through
1223	  the scalar tower \(R \to B_1 \to M\) then gives \(M\) module-finite over \(R\).
1224	\end{proof}
1225	
1226	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1227	[Descent of a localised-module structure along a scalar tower]
1228	  \label{lem:gf_islocalizedmodule_restrictscalars}
1229	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars}
1230	  Let \(A_g \to R\) be a ring map with compatible \(A_g\)- and \(R\)-module structures on
1231	  \(M\) and \(M'\) (scalar towers \(A_g \to R \to M\) and \(A_g \to R \to M'\)), and let
1232	  \(S \subseteq A_g\) be a multiplicative subset. If an \(R\)-linear map
1233	  \(f : M \to M'\) realises \(M'\) as the localisation of \(M\) at the image submonoid
1234	  \(S \cdot 1 \subseteq R\) over \(R\), then its restriction of scalars to \(A_g\) realises
1235	  \(M'\) as the localisation of \(M\) at \(S\) over \(A_g\).
1236	\end{lemma}
1237	\begin{proof}
1238	  One verifies the universal property of a localised module for the restricted map. Each
1239	  \(s \in S\) acts invertibly on \(M'\): its action coincides, via the scalar tower, with
1240	  that of its image in \(R\), which is a unit because \(f\) is a localisation at the image
1241	  submonoid. Surjectivity onto \(M'\) by fractions \(x/s\) with \(s \in S\) and the
1242	  equality criterion likewise descend from the corresponding image-submonoid statements,
1243	  rewriting each \(R\)-scalar by an \(A_g\)-scalar through the scalar tower. Assembling
1244	  these conditions gives the claim.
1245	\end{proof}
1246	
1247	\begin{lemma}[Localisation identification across an associated denominator]
1248	  \label{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated}
1249	  \lean{IsLocalization.Away.mul_of_associated}
1250	  \mathlibok
1251	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1252	  Let \(R \to S \to T\) be a tower of commutative rings with compatible structure maps, let
1253	  \(x, z \in R\) and \(y \in S\), and suppose \(S\) is the localisation of \(R\) away from
1254	  \(x\) and \(T\) is the localisation of \(S\) away from \(y\). If the image of \(z\) in \(S\)
1255	  is associated to \(y\) (the two differ by a unit), then \(T\) is the localisation of \(R\)
1256	  away from the single product \(x z\); that is, \(T \cong R[(x z)^{-1}]\).
1257	\end{lemma}

1259	\begin{lemma}[Freeness transports across a semilinear ring isomorphism]
1260	  \label{lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}
1261	  \lean{Module.Free.of_ringEquiv}
1262	  \mathlibok
1263	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1264	  Let \(R, R'\) be rings, \(M\) an \(R\)-module and \(M'\) an \(R'\)-module, and let
1265	  \(e : M \xrightarrow{\sim} M'\) be an additive isomorphism that is semilinear over a ring
1266	  isomorphism \(\sigma : R \xrightarrow{\sim} R'\), i.e.\ \(e(r \cdot m) = \sigma(r) \cdot e(m)\).
1267	  If \(M\) is free over \(R\), then \(M'\) is free over \(R'\).
1268	\end{lemma}

1270	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1271	[L5 step-4 helper — descent of freeness across a tower of \(\mathrm{Away}\) localisations]
1272	  \label{lem:gf_away_tower_descent}
1273	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.free_localizationAway_of_away_tower}
1274	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated, lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}
1275	  % LEAN DEPS: the localisation-transport primitives the Lean proof consumes are
1276	  %   IsLocalization.surj                          (clear the denominator of h over A_g)
1277	  %   IsLocalization.map_units                     (the cleared denominator s ∈ powers g is a unit)
1278	  %   IsLocalization.Away.mul_of_associated        (key ring identification: Away(g·a) on A_h)
1279	  %   IsLocalization.algEquiv                      (ring iso Away(g·a) ≅ A_h)
1280	  %   Module.Free.of_ringEquiv                     (transport freeness across that ring iso)
1281	  %   IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv                (the σ-semilinear module equivalence)
1282	  %   LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization    (upgrade the R-linear equiv to Away(g·a)-linear)
1283	  % The one primitive NOT packaged by Mathlib — that the composite localisation map
1284	  %   ψ := mkLinearMap(powers h) ∘ mkLinearMap(powers g) : T → (T_g)_h
1285	  % satisfies IsLocalizedModule (powers (g·a)) ψ — is proved directly from the three
1286	  % IsLocalizedModule axioms (units act invertibly, surjectivity up to denominators, the
1287	  % equality criterion); there is no packaged localisation-of-localisation lemma to invoke.
1288	  % NOTE: the closed Lean proof DID find a packaged route — the composite-localisation
1289	  % `IsLocalizedModule (powers (g·a)) ψ` step goes through `IsBaseChange.comp` via
1290	  % `isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange`, so the "no packaged lemma" remark above is superseded.
1291	  % WITNESS: the conclusion holds at the SINGLE product f := g·a (not at a square f²).
1292	  Let \(A\) be a domain and \(T\) an \(A\)-module. Let \(g \in A\), \(g \ne 0\), and let
1293	  \(h \in A_g := \mathrm{Away}(g)\) be non-zero. Write
1294	  \((T_g)_h\) for the iterated localisation of \(T\) — first away from \(g\), then away from
1295	  \(h\) — and \((A_g)_h := \mathrm{Away}(h)\) for the corresponding iterated localisation of the
1296	  base ring. If \((T_g)_h\) is free over \((A_g)_h\), then there exists \(f \in A\),
1297	  \(f \ne 0\), such that the single localisation \(T_f\) is free over \(A_f := \mathrm{Away}(f)\).
1298	  Concretely the witness is the single product \(f := g\,a\) for an element \(a \in A\) whose
1299	  image in \(A_g\) is associated to \(h\).
1300	\end{lemma}
1301	
1302	\begin{proof}
1303	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated, lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}
1304	  \emph{Clear the denominator of \(h\).} As \(h\) lies in \(A_g = \mathrm{Away}(g)\),
1305	  surjectivity of the localisation map \(A \to A_g\) onto fractions yields \(a \in A\) and a
1306	  power \(s\) of \(g\) with \(h \cdot \bar s = \bar a\) in \(A_g\), where \(\bar{\,\cdot\,}\)
1307	  denotes the image in \(A_g\). The image \(\bar s\) is a unit (a power of \(g\) is invertible
1308	  in \(A_g\)), so \(\bar a = h\,\bar s\) is associated to \(h\); in particular \(\bar a \ne 0\)
1309	  (because \(h \ne 0\)), hence \(a \ne 0\). Since \(A\) is a domain and \(g, a \ne 0\), the
1310	  product \(f := g\,a\) is non-zero.
1311	
1312	  \emph{Identify the base rings.} The associatedness of \(\bar a\) and \(h\), together with the
1313	  tower \(A \to A_g \to A_h\), identifies the iterated localisation
1314	  \((A_g)_h = \mathrm{Away}(h)\) with the single localisation of \(A\) away from the product
1315	  \(g\,a\): by \cref{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated} (with \(x = g\), \(z = a\),
1316	  \(y = h\)) the ring \(A_h\) is a localisation of \(A\) away from \(g\,a\), and uniqueness of
1317	  localisation furnishes a ring isomorphism \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a) \xrightarrow{\sim} A_h\).
1318	
1319	  \emph{Identify the modules.} The composite localisation map
1320	  \[
1321	    \psi : T \longrightarrow (T_g)_h, \qquad
1322	    \psi := \mathrm{mk}_{\langle h\rangle} \circ \mathrm{mk}_{\langle g\rangle},
1323	  \]
1324	  first into \(T_g\) and then into \((T_g)_h\), realises \((T_g)_h\) as the localisation of
1325	  \(T\) at the powers of \(g\,a\): every \(g^n a^m\) acts invertibly on \((T_g)_h\), \(\psi\) is
1326	  surjective up to such denominators, and its kernel is the expected one. This
1327	  localisation-of-localisation identity is verified directly from the defining properties of a
1328	  localised module; no packaged lemma supplies it.
1329	
1330	  \emph{Transport freeness.} By hypothesis \((T_g)_h\) is free over \(A_h\). The ring
1331	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a) \cong A_h\) and the induced semilinear module equivalence
1332	  \(T_{g\,a} \simeq (T_g)_h\) — assembled from the localised-module identification of the
1333	  previous step and promoted to an \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a)\)-linear equivalence — carry this
1334	  freeness back by \cref{lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}: \(T_{g\,a}\) is free over
1335	  \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a)\). Taking \(f := g\,a\) gives \(T_f\) free over \(A_f\), as required.
1336	\end{proof}

1338	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1339	[L5 — polynomial-ring core of generic freeness]
1340	  \label{lem:gf_polynomial_core}
1341	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial}
1342	  % NOTE (signature): the Lean decl uses a SHARED universe

1348	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module, lem:gf_splice_shortExact, lem:gf_torsion_base,
1349	        lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport, lem:gf_generic_rank_ses,
1350	        lem:gf_torsion_reindex, lem:gf_away_tower_descent}

1353	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1354	  % "Let $m$ be the generic rank of the finite module $B_g$ over the domain
1355	  %  $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$. Then we have a short exact sequence of

1357	  %  $0\to A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]^{\oplus m} \to B_g \to T \to 0$ where $T$ is a finite
1358	  %  torsion module over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$. Therefore, the dimension of the

1361	  %  there exists some $h\ne 0$ in $A$ with $T_h$ free over $A_{gh}$. Taking $f=gh$,

1363	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (polynomial-ring
1364	  core of the induction).}
1365	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain and \(d \ge 0\). Let \(N\) be a finite module over
1366	  the polynomial ring \(A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\), regarded as an \(A\)-module. Then there
1367	  exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that \(N_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
